text.title: Platform Engineering Examples
text.summary: Worked end-to-end example applying this methodology to a concrete scenario.

example.title: 80-dev SaaS adopts Backstage + Crossplane
example.code:
Org: acme (80 engineers, 38 services, 220 tickets/mo, TTFD 14d, NPS -5).
1. baseline-survey → top 3 complaints: 'cannot provision RDS without ticket', 'CI snippets diverge across teams', 'observability default missing'. 2. name-sponsor → CTO sponsors; non_goals_year1 = [multi-cloud, self-service DB]. 3. build-orchestration-backend → Crossplane + Terraform-controller; one AWS account; quota + RBAC. 4. ship-one-golden-path → Go microservice template (dominant shape: 24/38 services). Includes OTel sidecar, GitHub Actions CI, K8s manifests with PSS-restricted. 5. add-portal → Backstage catalogue + scaffolder action 'New Go Microservice'. 6. wire-metrics → dashboard shows DF, LT, MTTR, CFR + adoption_rate, ttfd_new_dev, ticket_trend, NPS. 7. publish → quarterly review at month 3: 11 services on golden path, TTFD down to 2 days, NPS +18, tickets -42 %. Path-v2 proposal: Node API golden path.
example.note: End-to-end trace through the 7-step procedure landing all 6 rules.
